what_it_does: This file is a wave-1 build map for creating 'recollection', an episodic-memory clone/refit plugin. It inventories four related codebases, lists the relevant file tree, identifies integration points and patterns, and assigns a KEEP / MODIFY / REPLACE / HARVEST disposition to every load-bearing piece so the build can proceed file-by-file.
observations: It inventories four codebases: the clone base /home/tim/episodic-memory, the integration target /home/tim/company, and two harvest sources (substrate-mcp and conversation-intelligence).
It assigns KEEP / MODIFY / REPLACE / HARVEST dispositions to files and symbols in the clone base.
It flags five highest-leverage touch points: paths.ts, db.ts, embeddings.ts, summarizer.ts, and mcp-server.ts.
It requires recollection to use its own data directory ~/.recollection so the corpus survives plugin updates.
It mandates that the MCP tool names search and read stay identical to episodic-memory for drop-in compatibility.
It plans to replace the in-process ONNX bge-small-384 embedder with a registry-driven, multi-lens OpenAI-compatible fabric embedding client.
It plans to replace the cloud Claude Agent SDK summarizer with a local resident-4B fabric distillation pipeline.
It extends the database schema with units, links, and multi-coordinate fingerprints while demoting the 384-dim vec_exchanges table to one lens among many.
It harvests substrate-mcp's state/temporal layer, k-means clustering, resolver ladder, chunker, and OpenAIEmbedder adapter.
It harvests conversation-intelligence's turn-context embedding builder, dual-granularity embedding config, artifact-provenance graph, parametric MCP tool design, and live-sync lane.
It follows an evidence convention of Observed / Inferred / Verified, and notes that no claims are Verified because there was no live execution this wave.
It repeatedly emphasizes registry-is-truth, no hardcoding, no versioning, commit-to-main, fail-loud behavior, and 'bge-m3 has zero priority'.
standouts: The core thesis that the chassis is sound but the engine is wrong: keep the capture/sync/plugin skeleton, replace the embedder, summarizer, and flat search.
The durability fix of moving the data directory to ~/.recollection so plugin updates do not relocate the corpus.
The multi-lens fingerprint design where no single embedding model or dimension is the winner.
The parametric MCP verb design (one tool with op/scope/axis/return_format selectors) matching both the Company and conversation-intelligence house styles.
The substrate-mcp state-asymmetry sensor is called the 'crown jewel' to port for recollection's HEALTH/temporal layer.
concerns: Many exact harvest locations are 'subagent-verified' rather than directly observed by the author, which could introduce location drift.
Several design decisions are explicitly left open for Tim (lens loadout, Codex harness keep/drop, marketplace publish, standalone-vs-absorbed timing).
A large portion of the absorption mappings are Inferred and may need rework once actual integration begins.
The mix of Observed and Inferred evidence levels across hundreds of claims could make later audit difficult.
No claims are Verified because no live execution was performed this wave.
notes: This is the wave-1 build map that decides, file-by-file, what to keep, modify, replace, and harvest while forking episodic-memory into the recollection plugin.
questions: What is the initial lens/model loadout and registry format for the multi-lens embedder?
How will the ~/.recollection directory be initialized and backfilled from the existing episodic-memory archive?
What is the planned order of standalone build milestones versus Company absorption?
How will the parametric MCP tool schema be validated in TypeScript?
What is the fallback behavior when local fabric services are not resident?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to provide a build-level map for recollection, and the content delivers per-codebase file trees, integration points, dispositions, and harvest sources.
apparent_themes: Multi-lens embeddings with no single winner
Provenance graph and state-asymmetry as memory metabolism
intention_signals: It is trying to provide a concrete, file-level execution plan for building recollection as a drop-in sibling plugin that can later fold into the Company, while deliberately harvesting proven designs from substrate-mcp and conversation-intelligence rather than inventing them.
novelty: It is a build map that assigns explicit KEEP/MODIFY/REPLACE/HARVEST dispositions to every load-bearing file of a fork, explicitly designed for later absorption into a larger system rather than immediate integration.
contribution: It contributes a structured inventory of which existing code pieces to reuse, adapt, or replace, plus a curated list of exact source locations and patterns to port, giving a fused system a reusable migration blueprint and decision record.
summary_for_embedding: Wave-1 build map for forking episodic-memory into a standalone recollection plugin, listing per-file KEEP/MODIFY/REPLACE/HARVEST dispositions, Company integration patterns to match, and exact harvest targets from substrate-mcp and conversation-intelligence.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a file-level execution plan that makes recollection a drop-in, update-surviving episodic-memory replacement built on local fabric and ready for later Company absorption.
